name: Glazed Pork Chops with Pickled Ginger
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------CHOPS-----------------------------------
1/4 c Hoisin sauce
2 tbsp Ketchup
2 tbsp White vinegar
2 tbsp Reduced-sodium soy sauce
4 Pork loin chops, 1/2 to 3/4-inch thick, trimmed
Salt and cracked black pepper
3 Green onions, sliced thin diagonally
Hoisin sauce is available in Asian markets and in the specialty section of many supermarkets.
1. Make Pickled Ginger: Heat vinegar, sugar, and salt in a small saucepan over medium heat until boiling. Add ginger; return to boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 10 minutes. Cool completely. Cover and refrigerate (can be made up to one month ahead). Alternatively, use pre-pickled ginger found in Asian markets or the Asian food section of supermarkets.
2. Heat grill.
3. Combine hoisin sauce, ketchup, white vinegar, and soy sauce in a small bowl. Set aside.
4. Sprinkle chops with salt and pepper. Grill over medium-hot heat for 3 to 4 minutes per side. During the last 30 seconds, brush tops with hoisin mixture and grill for an additional 15 seconds. Turn, brush the other side with hoisin, and grill for another 15 seconds.
5. Transfer chops to serving plates and sprinkle with green onions. Serve pickled ginger on the side.
PER SERVING Calories 345 Total Fat 12 g Saturated Fat 4 g Cholesterol 114 mg Sodium 973 mg Carbohydrates 15 g Protein 44 g
